item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

really cool, reminds me of SCP-3999

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing I disliked about this was Monroe's monologue explaining to us what was already implicit, but aside from that, 10/10 SCP. This is one of, if not THE best, series VI article I've read.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good shit.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually heard this one first as a reading before reading it myself. I feel I got a lot more from reading it myself, and I loved it! In all honesty, I didn't realize it was an exquisite corpse scip until I saw the tag, and expected it to be one for the 5000 slot contest, as it is a great mystery in itself! Loved it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, this is EXTREMELY GOOD. The buildup is exquisite, the formatting's great and I LOVE the video log. Also love your source of inspiration- those are excellent games!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First guess: The static is a meme. He specifically checks everyone's dialogue against the meme-detector, but not the static.

Update- Shit, I was wrong. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now this is fantastic, I absolutely love the payoff at the end, it's creepy, it's weird, it's well written, what more could you ask for?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, good catch :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incredibly wild ride. +1.

I also corrected "Monroe is makes various noises" to "Monroe makes various noises" in the bit where he enters the halls. Wonderful work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding. The containment procedures work, the opening notes work, and everything from the dream onward stumbles.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Yossi is holding my family hostage unless I upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent article so you already got my +1.

My problem was a possible technical error. I'm reading on a phone and the line, "Official inquiry request" and subsequent line are half vertically merged with one another. 9/10 times it's the phone misinterpreting what's there and turning landscape usually confirms it's the phone but nothing changed. It might be worth a quick look at the bookends for a remnant font or line space/break that should or should not be there.

Normally I'd do the leg work but I'm on a phone and I despise trying to do anything with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-5545-3 is a phenomenon resulting in SCP-5545-2's interactions with SCP-5545-1. During this process, SCP-5545-1's area of effect will exponentially increase with time, resulting in the manifestation of SCP-5545-1 occurring globally within a period of 4–6 hours. This will continue so as long as SCP-5545-2 is present within SCP-5545-1; subversively, SCP-5545-1 will logarithmically decrease in its area of effect while SCP-5545-2 is not present within it.

Okay, but if it can only be entered via Site-344-1, why does this matter? How do they know it extends globally if there's only one entrance and exit?
(also I think you meant "resulting from")
The use of "arctic" was odd, given that it's in Antarctica. Also, the references to "night" and "day" by Monroe (in November there it's light for 24 hours a day), though that could be explained by an artificial day-night lighting cycle within the site.

Regarding the reincarnation: does Monroe reincarnate as an adult of the same age every time? Speaking the same language? Does he have a memetic effect that everyone (including himself) has memories of his life up to the moment he "appeared"? Or he retroactively always existed? Does that mean the murder he committed happens again and again? Does he just appear out of whole cloth, or does the body he inhabits exist as someone else until it's time, morphing physically and mentally to become Jason Monroe?

One more typo:

made me realize was I was standing in front of

item.title: Re: Coauthor Post
item.content:encoded: They’re great! I can see how they led to inspiring this full article or a bunch of other ideas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like most of it, except the final bit from O5. It's really well put together.

First, though: "logarithmically decrease" what does that mean? Should this be "exponentially decay"?

I have two issues with the final part: it's got a "voiceover explains everything" feel to it — it feels like a cheap way to tie up loose ends.

Second — the quip at the end just really annoyed me. The Foundation does research all the time, to better execute its mission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I kind of clued into how Monroe was going to be revealed as the anomaly, it was a very entertaining ride along the way to how things turned out.

One note: I do feel that the last email could be greatly reduced or eliminated all together as it lessens the surreal reveal at the end, but it's not a huge sticking point. +1

item.title: Coauthor Post
item.content:encoded:
So to begin, I was so ecstatic to learn that Yossi had gotten my ConProcs! He's a wonderful author and I'm a big fan of him, so I knew this was gonna be great1.

Regarding the ConProcs themselves, I think they're my favorite I've ever written. I wanted to be sure I would write something that inspired something interesting within the other author, without forcing them into a box. I had a lot of fun with this idea, and I think the idea of a conceptual site was my favorite, and I'm glad Yossi used it so well.

Footnotes
1. And I was right!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck is going on???

Mood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article has a very strong opening that’s ultimately bogged down by unconvincingly spooky dreams and an ending that SCP-5683 executed much more crisply.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has a nice mystery, with a hint of something bigger going on at the end.

Theory
What Might be Going on...

Reincarnating man under punishment for murder, never remembering what he has done till he enters SCP-5545-4. But given that the author stated "Lobotomy Corporation" and "Library of Ruina" as influences, I think it involves something biblical.

item.title: Great work!!!
item.content:encoded: I read through several stages of this draft, and each one pretty much kept me enthralled. The final version is great as well, giving you just enough closure but also still leaving some of the mystery up in the air. Great article, I can't wait to see what you've got next in store! You've done some great work here! Enthusiastic +1

item.title: Alternative Title: "String Theocracy"
item.content:encoded:
Cut it off, you've already lost!
All that precious bravery is gonna get you hurt,
In a world that feeds on the minority.
May that self-centered belief lead you to peace!
If you're gonna replace me,
At least have the audacity to kill me thoroughly.

Heya! This is an experimental piece for me, so I'm hoping that I managed to get some things right. This was heavily inspired by Lobotomy Corporation and its sequel Library of Ruina, specifically the term "abnormality," this song from Library of Ruina (as well as String Theocracy, which I quoted above), and the symbolism used in both games. Give both of those a look!

Thank you to everyone who helped me write this, including:

  • Aethris, who heavily encouraged me to continue writing.
  • JackalRelated, who gave some early-on critique and indirectly helped pushed me to write.
  • Popsioak, JustBixby, pr0m37h3um, Brewsterion 1017, GerrymanderBassist, KaraKatt, Gamesofmax, Elunerazim, and Heckhead for critique and feedback.

Hope you enjoyed reading! If you like my works, you can find more on my authorphage.

hallway.JPG

intotheabyss.jpg

sheiswatching.png
